reasoning: The resolution criteria specify that the market will resolve according to the total customs duties collected in FY 2025, as reported in the Financial Report of the U.S. Government. Fiscal Year 2025 ended on September 30, 2025. Recent reporting on the Treasury's year-end summaries (e.g., the final Monthly Treasury Statement data released in October 2025) indicates that despite a surge due to new tariffs, total FY 2025 customs duties were approximately $195 billion. Since this figure is finalized for the fiscal year and is vastly lower than the $500 billion minimum required for this bracket, there is virtually zero chance the upcoming formal Financial Report will revise customs duties up by over $300 billion. Thus, the probability of the U.S. collecting between $500B and $1T in customs duties for FY 2025 is effectively zero.
